388	    @staticmethod
389	    def _is_cancelled_result(trial_result: TrialResult) -> bool:
390	        return (
391	            trial_result.exception_info is not None
392	            and trial_result.exception_info.exception_type == CANCELLED_ERROR_TYPE
393	        )
394	
395	    def _refresh_job_progress(
396	        self,
397	        *,
398	        updated_at: datetime | None = None,
399	    ) -> None:
400	        updated_at = updated_at or datetime.now()
401	        self._job_result.updated_at = updated_at
402	        self._job_result.stats.update_progress_counts(
403	            n_total_trials=self._job_result.n_total_trials,
404	            n_running_trials=len(self._running_trial_ids),
405	            n_cancelled_trials=len(self._cancelled_trial_ids),
406	            n_retries=self._n_retries,
407	        )
408	
409	    @staticmethod
410	    def _evals_key_for_result(trial_result: TrialResult) -> tuple[str, str]:
411	        agent_name = trial_result.agent_info.name
412	        model_name = (
413	            trial_result.agent_info.model_info.name
414	            if trial_result.agent_info.model_info
415	            else None
416	        )
417	        dataset_name = trial_result.source or "adhoc"
418	        return (
419	            JobStats.format_agent_evals_key(agent_name, model_name, dataset_name),
420	            dataset_name,
421	        )
422	
423	    def _refresh_metrics_for_eval(self, evals_key: str, dataset_name: str) -> None:
424	        rewards_list = list(self._live_rewards[evals_key].values())
425	        if not rewards_list:
426	            self._job_result.stats.evals[evals_key].metrics = []
427	            return
428	        self._job_result.stats.evals[evals_key].metrics = [
429	            metric.compute(rewards_list) for metric in self._metrics[dataset_name]
430	        ]
431	
432	    def _remove_completed_attempt_for_retry(self, trial_name: str) -> None:
433	        previous_result = self._previous_trial_results.pop(trial_name, None)
434	        if previous_result is None:
435	            return
436	
437	        evals_key, dataset_name = self._evals_key_for_result(previous_result)
438	        self._live_rewards[evals_key].pop(trial_name, None)
439	        self._job_result.stats.remove_trial(previous_result)
440	        if self._is_cancelled_result(previous_result):
441	            self._cancelled_trial_ids.discard(trial_name)
442	        self._n_retries += 1
443	        self._refresh_metrics_for_eval(evals_key, dataset_name)
444	
445	    def _write_job_result(self, *, exclude_trial_results: bool = False) -> None:
446	        if exclude_trial_results:
447	            result_json = self._job_result.model_dump_json(
448	                indent=4,
449	                exclude={"trial_results"},
450	            )
451	        else:
452	            result_json = self._job_result.model_dump_json(indent=4)
453	        self._job_result_path.write_text(result_json)
454	
455	    async def _write_job_result_async(
456	        self, *, exclude_trial_results: bool = False
457	    ) -> None:
458	        await asyncio.to_thread(
459	            self._write_job_result,
460	            exclude_trial_results=exclude_trial_results,
461	        )

463	    @staticmethod
464	    async def _resolve_metrics(
465	        config: JobConfig, task_configs: list[TaskConfig]
466	    ) -> dict[str, list[BaseMetric[Any]]]:
467	        metrics: dict[str, list[BaseMetric[Any]]] = defaultdict(list)
468	
469	        job_metrics = [
470	            MetricFactory.create_metric(metric.type, **metric.kwargs)
471	            for metric in config.metrics
472	        ]
473	
474	        metrics["adhoc"].extend(job_metrics)
475	
476	        for dataset_config in config.datasets:
477	            await Job._resolve_dataset_metrics(dataset_config, metrics, job_metrics)
478	
479	        for name, metric_list in metrics.items():
480	            if len(metric_list) == 0:
481	                metrics[name].append(Mean())
482	
483	        return metrics
484	
485	    @staticmethod
486	    async def _resolve_dataset_metrics(
487	        dataset_config: DatasetConfig,
488	        metrics: dict[str, list[BaseMetric[Any]]],
489	        job_metrics: list[BaseMetric[Any]],
490	    ) -> None:
491	        if dataset_config.is_repo():
492	            from harbor.registry.client.factory import RegistryClientFactory
493	
494	            if dataset_config.repo is None:
495	                raise RuntimeError(
496	                    "Repo dataset config is missing repo; this should never happen."
497	                )
498	            client = RegistryClientFactory.create(
499	                repo=dataset_config.repo,
500	                path=dataset_config.path,
501	                registry_path=dataset_config.registry_path,
502	            )
503	            if dataset_config.name is not None:
504	                name_string = (
505	                    f"{dataset_config.name}@{dataset_config.version}"
506	                    if dataset_config.version
507	                    else dataset_config.name
508	                )
509	            else:
510	                name_string = ""
511	            metadata = await client.get_dataset_metadata(name_string)
512	            metrics[metadata.name].extend(
513	                [
514	                    MetricFactory.create_metric(metric.type, **metric.kwargs)
515	                    for metric in metadata.metrics
516	                ]
517	            )
518	            metrics[metadata.name].extend(job_metrics)
519	        elif dataset_config.is_local():
520	            if dataset_config.path is None:
521	                raise RuntimeError(
522	                    "Local dataset config is missing path; this should never happen."
523	                )
524	            source = dataset_config.path.expanduser().resolve().name
525	            metrics[source].extend(job_metrics)
526	        elif dataset_config.is_package():
527	            from harbor.registry.client.package import PackageDatasetClient
528	
529	            if dataset_config.name is None:
530	                raise RuntimeError(
531	                    "Package dataset config is missing name; this should never happen."
532	                )
533	            client = PackageDatasetClient()
534	            name_string = f"{dataset_config.name}@{dataset_config.ref or 'latest'}"
535	            metadata = await client.get_dataset_metadata(name_string)
536	
537	            # Download dataset files (e.g., metric.py)
538	            downloaded_files = await client.download_dataset_files(metadata)
539	            if DatasetPaths.METRIC_FILENAME in downloaded_files:
540	                from harbor.metrics.uv_script import UvScript
541	
542	                metrics[dataset_config.name].append(
543	                    UvScript(script_path=downloaded_files[DatasetPaths.METRIC_FILENAME])
544	                )
545	
546	            metrics[dataset_config.name].extend(
547	                [
548	                    MetricFactory.create_metric(metric.type, **metric.kwargs)
549	                    for metric in metadata.metrics
550	                ]
551	            )
552	            metrics[dataset_config.name].extend(job_metrics)
553	        elif dataset_config.is_registry():
554	            if dataset_config.name is None:
555	                raise RuntimeError(
556	                    "Registry dataset config is missing name; this should never happen."
557	                )
558	            from harbor.registry.client.factory import RegistryClientFactory
559	
560	            client = RegistryClientFactory.create(
561	                registry_url=dataset_config.registry_url,
562	                registry_path=dataset_config.registry_path,
563	            )
564	            name_string = (
565	                f"{dataset_config.name}@{dataset_config.version}"
566	                if dataset_config.version
567	                else dataset_config.name
568	            )
569	            metadata = await client.get_dataset_metadata(name_string)
570	            metrics[dataset_config.name].extend(
571	                [
572	                    MetricFactory.create_metric(metric.type, **metric.kwargs)
573	                    for metric in metadata.metrics
574	                ]
575	            )
576	            metrics[dataset_config.name].extend(job_metrics)
577	
578	    @staticmethod
579	    async def _cache_tasks(
580	        task_configs: list[TaskConfig],
581	    ) -> dict[TaskIdType, TaskDownloadResult]:
582	        """Pre-download git-based tasks before submitting trials."""
583	        remote_configs = [
584	            config
585	            for config in task_configs
586	            if config.is_git_task() or config.is_package_task()
587	        ]
588	        if not remote_configs:
589	            return {}
590	
591	        overwrites = {config.overwrite for config in remote_configs}
592	        output_dirs = {config.download_dir for config in remote_configs}
593	
594	        if len(overwrites) > 1 or len(output_dirs) > 1:
595	            raise ValueError(
596	                "overwrite and output_dir cannot be different for different trials. "
597	                "This should never happen."
598	            )
599	
600	        client = TaskClient()
601	
602	        task_ids = [config.get_task_id() for config in remote_configs]
603	        result = await client.download_tasks(
604	            task_ids=task_ids,
605	            overwrite=any(overwrites),
606	            output_dir=output_dirs.pop() if output_dirs else None,
607	        )
608	
609	        return dict(zip(task_ids, result.results))
610	
611	    def _init_job_lock(self) -> None:
612	        self._job_lock = build_job_lock(
613	            config=self.config,
614	            trial_configs=self._trial_configs,
615	            task_download_results=self._task_download_results,
616	        )
617	
618	    def _write_job_lock(self) -> None:
619	        if self._job_lock is None:
620	            self._init_job_lock()
621	        if self._job_lock is None:
622	            raise RuntimeError(
623	                "Job lock was not initialized after _init_job_lock(); "
624	                "this should never happen."
625	            )
626	        if self._job_lock_path.exists():
627	            existing_job_lock = self._read_existing_job_lock()
628	            if existing_job_lock is None:
629	                raise ValueError(
630	                    f"Existing lock file at {self._job_lock_path} could not be "
631	                    "parsed; refusing to overwrite it."
632	                )
633	            self._job_lock.created_at = existing_job_lock.created_at
634	            self._job_lock.harbor = existing_job_lock.harbor
635	            self._job_lock.invocation = existing_job_lock.invocation
636	            if existing_job_lock != self._job_lock:
637	                raise FileExistsError(
638	                    f"Job directory {self.job_dir} already has a lock.json that "
639	                    "does not match the resolved job lock."
640	                )
641	        self._job_lock_path.write_text(
642	            self._job_lock.model_dump_json(indent=4, exclude_none=True)
643	        )
644	
645	    def _read_existing_job_lock(self) -> JobLock | None:
646	        try:
647	            return JobLock.model_validate_json(self._job_lock_path.read_text())
648	        except Exception:
649	            return None
650	
651	    async def _on_trial_started(self, event: TrialHookEvent) -> None:
652	        """Internal hook to update live progress when a trial starts."""
653	        async with self._trial_completion_lock:
654	            self._remove_completed_attempt_for_retry(event.trial_id)
655	            self._running_trial_ids.add(event.trial_id)
656	            self._refresh_job_progress(updated_at=event.timestamp)
657	            await self._write_job_result_async(exclude_trial_results=True)
658	
659	    async def _on_trial_cancelled(self, event: TrialHookEvent) -> None:
660	        """Internal hook to update live progress when cancellation is requested."""
661	        async with self._trial_completion_lock:
662	            self._cancelled_trial_ids.add(event.trial_id)
663	            self._refresh_job_progress(updated_at=event.timestamp)
664	            await self._write_job_result_async(exclude_trial_results=True)
665	
666	    async def _on_trial_completed(self, event: TrialHookEvent) -> None:
667	        """Internal hook to update job stats when a trial completes."""
668	        if event.result is None:
669	            raise ValueError(
670	                f"Trial {event.trial_id} completed without a result. "
671	                "This should never happen."
672	            )
673	
674	        trial_result = event.result
675	        trial_name = trial_result.trial_name
676	
677	        async with self._trial_completion_lock:
678	            previous_result = self._previous_trial_results.get(trial_name)
679	
680	            evals_key, dataset_name = self._evals_key_for_result(trial_result)
681	
682	            self._live_rewards[evals_key][trial_name] = (
683	                trial_result.verifier_result.rewards
684	                if trial_result.verifier_result is not None
685	                else None
686	            )
687	
688	            self._job_result.stats.update_trial(
689	                new_result=trial_result,
690	                previous_result=previous_result,
691	            )
692	
693	            self._previous_trial_results[trial_name] = trial_result
694	
695	            self._running_trial_ids.discard(trial_name)
696	            if previous_result is not None and self._is_cancelled_result(
697	                previous_result
698	            ):
699	                self._cancelled_trial_ids.discard(trial_name)
700	            if self._is_cancelled_result(trial_result):
701	                self._cancelled_trial_ids.add(trial_name)
702	
703	            self._refresh_metrics_for_eval(evals_key, dataset_name)
704	
705	            self._refresh_job_progress(updated_at=event.timestamp)
706	            await self._write_job_result_async(exclude_trial_results=True)
707	
708	    async def run(self) -> JobResult:
709	        """Run all configured trials and return the final aggregated job result."""
710	        try:
711	            started_at = (
712	                self._existing_job_result.started_at
713	                if self._existing_job_result is not None
714	                else datetime.now()
715	            )
716	            self._job_result = JobResult(
717	                id=self._id,
718	                started_at=started_at,
719	                updated_at=started_at,
720	                n_total_trials=len(self._trial_configs),
721	                stats=JobStats.from_trial_results(
722	                    self._existing_trial_results,
723	                    n_total_trials=len(self._trial_configs),
724	                    n_retries=self._n_retries,
725	                ),
726	            )
727	            self._refresh_job_progress()
728	
729	            self._job_config_path.write_text(self.config.model_dump_json(indent=4))
730	            self._init_job_lock()
731	            self._write_job_lock()
732	            self._write_job_result(exclude_trial_results=True)
733	
734	            # Set up progress UI and register progress hooks
735	            n_remaining = len(self._remaining_trial_configs)
736	            n_existing = len(self._existing_trial_configs)
737	
738	            loading_progress = Progress(
739	                SpinnerColumn(),
740	                MofNCompleteColumn(),
741	                TextColumn("[progress.description]{task.description}"),
742	                BarColumn(),
743	                TimeElapsedColumn(),
744	                TimeRemainingColumn(),
745	                redirect_stdout=False,
746	                redirect_stderr=False,
747	            )
748	
749	            if self.config.quiet:
750	                with loading_progress:
751	                    progress_task = loading_progress.add_task(
752	                        "Running trials...",
753	                        total=n_remaining + n_existing,
754	                        completed=n_existing,
755	                    )
756	
757	                    trial_results = await self._run_trials_with_queue(
758	                        loading_progress, progress_task
759	                    )
760	            else:
761	                running_progress = Progress(
762	                    SpinnerColumn(),
763	                    TimeElapsedColumn(),
764	                    TextColumn("[progress.description]{task.description}"),
765	                    redirect_stdout=False,
766	                    redirect_stderr=False,
767	                )
768	
769	                with Live(
770	                    Group(loading_progress, running_progress),
771	                    refresh_per_second=10,
772	                    redirect_stdout=False,
773	                    redirect_stderr=False,
774	                ):
775	                    progress_task = loading_progress.add_task(
776	                        "Running trials...",
777	                        total=n_remaining + n_existing,
778	                        completed=n_existing,
779	                    )
780	
781	                    trial_results = await self._run_trials_with_queue(
782	                        loading_progress, progress_task, running_progress
783	                    )
784	
785	            combined_trial_results = self._existing_trial_results + trial_results
786	
787	            final_rewards = defaultdict(list)
788	
789	            for trial_result in combined_trial_results:
790	                agent_name = trial_result.agent_info.name
791	                model_name = (
792	                    trial_result.agent_info.model_info.name
793	                    if trial_result.agent_info.model_info
794	                    else None
795	                )
796	                dataset_name = trial_result.source or "adhoc"
797	                evals_key = JobStats.format_agent_evals_key(
798	                    agent_name, model_name, dataset_name
799	                )
800	
801	                if trial_result.verifier_result is not None:
802	                    final_rewards[evals_key].append(
803	                        trial_result.verifier_result.rewards
804	                    )
805	                else:
806	                    final_rewards[evals_key].append(None)
807	
808	            final_stats = JobStats.from_trial_results(
809	                combined_trial_results,
810	                n_total_trials=len(self._trial_configs),
811	                n_retries=self._n_retries,
812	            )
813	
814	            for evals_key, rewards in final_rewards.items():
815	                # Extract dataset_name from the evals_key to get the right metrics
816	                # The key format is either "agent__model__dataset" or "agent__dataset"
817	                dataset_name = evals_key.split("__")[-1]
818	                for metric in self._metrics[dataset_name]:
819	                    final_stats.evals[evals_key].metrics.append(metric.compute(rewards))
820	
821	            for evals_key, pass_at_k in compute_pass_at_k_by_evals(
822	                combined_trial_results
823	            ).items():
824	                final_stats.evals[evals_key].pass_at_k = pass_at_k
825	
826	            self._job_result.stats = final_stats
827	            self._job_result.trial_results = combined_trial_results
828	
829	            finished_at = datetime.now()
830	            self._job_result.finished_at = finished_at
831	            self._refresh_job_progress(updated_at=finished_at)
832	            self._write_job_result(exclude_trial_results=True)
833	
834	            return self._job_result
835	        finally:
836	            self._close_logger_handlers()
837	
838	    async def _run_trials_with_queue(
839	        self,
840	        loading_progress: Progress,
841	        loading_progress_task,
842	        running_progress: Progress | None = None,
843	    ) -> list[TrialResult]:
844	        """Submit trials to the queue and collect results."""
845	        # Track per-trial progress tasks for the running_progress display.
846	        # Guards ensure hooks firing on retry attempts are idempotent.
847	        trial_progress_tasks: dict[str, TaskID] = {}
848	        advanced_trials: set[str] = set()
849	
850	        if running_progress is not None:
851	
852	            async def on_start(event: TrialHookEvent):
853	                if event.trial_id not in trial_progress_tasks:
854	                    task_id = running_progress.add_task(
855	                        f"{event.trial_id}: running trial...", total=None
856	                    )
857	                    trial_progress_tasks[event.trial_id] = task_id
858	
859	            async def on_environment_start(event: TrialHookEvent):
860	                if event.trial_id in trial_progress_tasks:
861	                    running_progress.update(
862	                        trial_progress_tasks[event.trial_id],
863	                        description=f"{event.trial_id}: starting environment...",
864	                    )
865	
866	            async def on_agent_start(event: TrialHookEvent):
867	                if event.trial_id in trial_progress_tasks:
868	                    running_progress.update(
869	                        trial_progress_tasks[event.trial_id],
870	                        description=f"{event.trial_id}: running agent...",
871	                    )
872	
873	            async def on_verification_start(event: TrialHookEvent):
874	                if event.trial_id in trial_progress_tasks:
875	                    running_progress.update(
876	                        trial_progress_tasks[event.trial_id],
877	                        description=f"{event.trial_id}: running verifier...",
878	                    )
879	
880	            async def on_cancel(event: TrialHookEvent):
881	                if event.trial_id in trial_progress_tasks:
882	                    running_progress.update(
883	                        trial_progress_tasks[event.trial_id],
884	                        description=f"{event.trial_id}: canceling trial; "
885	                        "this may take up to a minute...",
886	                    )
887	
888	            async def on_end_progress(event: TrialHookEvent):
889	                if event.trial_id in trial_progress_tasks:
890	                    running_progress.remove_task(
891	                        trial_progress_tasks.pop(event.trial_id)
892	                    )
893	                if event.trial_id not in advanced_trials:
894	                    advanced_trials.add(event.trial_id)
895	                    loading_progress.advance(loading_progress_task)
896	                    self._update_metric_display(
897	                        event, loading_progress, loading_progress_task
898	                    )
899	
900	            self.add_hook(TrialEvent.START, on_start)
901	            self.add_hook(TrialEvent.ENVIRONMENT_START, on_environment_start)
902	            self.add_hook(TrialEvent.AGENT_START, on_agent_start)
903	            self.add_hook(TrialEvent.VERIFICATION_START, on_verification_start)
904	            self.add_hook(TrialEvent.CANCEL, on_cancel)
905	            self.add_hook(TrialEvent.END, on_end_progress)
906	        else:
907	
908	            async def on_end_quiet(event: TrialHookEvent):
909	                if event.trial_id not in advanced_trials:
910	                    advanced_trials.add(event.trial_id)
911	                    loading_progress.advance(loading_progress_task)
912	                    self._update_metric_display(
913	                        event, loading_progress, loading_progress_task
914	                    )
915	
916	            self.add_hook(TrialEvent.END, on_end_quiet)
917	
918	        coros = self._trial_queue.submit_batch(self._remaining_trial_configs)
919	
920	        async with asyncio.TaskGroup() as tg:
921	            tasks = [tg.create_task(coro) for coro in coros]
922	
923	        return [t.result() for t in tasks]
924	
925	    def _update_metric_display(
926	        self, event: TrialHookEvent, loading_progress, loading_progress_task
927	    ):
928	        """Update the progress bar description with the latest metric value."""
929	        if not self._metrics or event.result is None:
930	            return
931	        dataset_name = event.config.task.source or "adhoc"
932	        if dataset_name not in self._metrics:
933	            return
934	
935	        rewards = list(
936	            self._live_rewards.get(
937	                JobStats.format_agent_evals_key(
938	                    event.result.agent_info.name,
939	                    event.result.agent_info.model_info.name
940	                    if event.result.agent_info.model_info
941	                    else None,
942	                    dataset_name,
943	                ),
944	                {},
945	            ).values()
946	        )
947	
948	        if rewards:
949	            metric_result = self._metrics[dataset_name][0].compute(rewards)
950	            first_metric_name, first_metric_value = next(iter(metric_result.items()))
951	            loading_progress.update(
952	                loading_progress_task,
953	                description=f"{first_metric_name.title()}: {first_metric_value:.3f}",
954	            )

1	import asyncio
2	import shutil
3	from collections.abc import Coroutine
4	from dataclasses import dataclass
5	from typing import Any
6	
7	from harbor.models.job.config import RetryConfig
8	from harbor.models.trial.config import TrialConfig
9	from harbor.models.trial.result import TrialResult
10	from harbor.trial.hooks import HookCallback, TrialEvent, TrialHookEvent
11	from harbor.utils.logger import logger
12	
13	
14	@dataclass(frozen=True)
15	class _AgentSemaphorePermit:
16	    semaphores: tuple[asyncio.Semaphore, ...]
17	
18	
19	class TrialQueue:
20	    """
21	    Handles orchestration of concurrent trials.
22	
23	    Receives TrialConfigs, creates Trial objects internally, runs them
24	    with retry logic, and returns TrialResult tasks. Concurrency is
25	    bounded by an asyncio.Semaphore. Hooks are wired to each Trial
26	    instance — Trial handles all event invocations.
27	    """
28	
29	    def __init__(
30	        self,
31	        n_concurrent: int,
32	        retry_config: RetryConfig | None = None,
33	        hooks: dict[TrialEvent, list[HookCallback]] | None = None,
34	    ):
35	        if hooks is None:
36	            hooks = {event: [] for event in TrialEvent}
37	        else:
38	            for event in TrialEvent:
39	                hooks.setdefault(event, [])
40	
41	        self._n_concurrent = n_concurrent
42	        self._retry_config = retry_config if retry_config is not None else RetryConfig()
43	        self._hooks = hooks
44	        self._logger = logger.getChild(__name__)
45	        self._semaphore = asyncio.Semaphore(n_concurrent)
46	        self._agent_pool_limits: dict[str, int] = {}
47	        self._agent_pool_semaphores: dict[str, asyncio.Semaphore] = {}
48	        self._held_agent_permits: dict[str, list[_AgentSemaphorePermit]] = {}
49	
50	    def add_hook(self, event: TrialEvent, callback: HookCallback) -> "TrialQueue":
51	        """Register a callback for a trial lifecycle event and return the queue."""
52	        self._hooks[event].append(callback)
53	        return self
54	
55	    def on_trial_started(self, callback: HookCallback) -> "TrialQueue":
56	        """Register a callback that runs when a queued trial starts."""
57	        return self.add_hook(TrialEvent.START, callback)
58	
59	    def on_environment_started(self, callback: HookCallback) -> "TrialQueue":
60	        """Register a callback that runs when a trial environment starts."""
61	        return self.add_hook(TrialEvent.ENVIRONMENT_START, callback)
62	
63	    def on_agent_started(self, callback: HookCallback) -> "TrialQueue":
64	        """Register a callback that runs when a trial agent starts."""
65	        return self.add_hook(TrialEvent.AGENT_START, callback)
66	
67	    def on_agent_ended(self, callback: HookCallback) -> "TrialQueue":
68	        """Register a callback that runs when a trial agent ends."""
69	        return self.add_hook(TrialEvent.AGENT_END, callback)
70	
71	    def on_verification_started(self, callback: HookCallback) -> "TrialQueue":
72	        """Register a callback that runs when trial verification starts."""
73	        return self.add_hook(TrialEvent.VERIFICATION_START, callback)
74	
75	    def on_trial_ended(self, callback: HookCallback) -> "TrialQueue":
76	        """Register a callback that runs when a queued trial ends."""
77	        return self.add_hook(TrialEvent.END, callback)
78	
79	    def on_trial_cancelled(self, callback: HookCallback) -> "TrialQueue":
80	        """Register a callback that runs when a queued trial is cancelled."""
81	        return self.add_hook(TrialEvent.CANCEL, callback)
82	
83	    def _should_retry_exception(self, exception_type: str) -> bool:
84	        """Check if an exception should trigger a retry."""
85	        if (
86	            self._retry_config.exclude_exceptions
87	            and exception_type in self._retry_config.exclude_exceptions
88	        ):
89	            self._logger.debug(
90	                f"Exception {exception_type} is in exclude_exceptions, not retrying"
91	            )
92	            return False
93	
94	        if (
95	            self._retry_config.include_exceptions
96	            and exception_type not in self._retry_config.include_exceptions
97	        ):
98	            self._logger.debug(
99	                f"Exception {exception_type} is not in include_exceptions, not retrying"
100	            )
101	            return False
102	
103	        return True
104	
105	    def _calculate_backoff_delay_sec(self, attempt: int) -> float:
106	        """Calculate the backoff delay for a retry attempt."""
107	        delay_sec = self._retry_config.min_wait_sec * (
108	            self._retry_config.wait_multiplier**attempt
109	        )
110	        return min(delay_sec, self._retry_config.max_wait_sec)
111	
112	    def _setup_hooks(self, trial) -> None:
113	        """Wire queue-level hooks to the trial."""
114	        if self._uses_agent_concurrency(trial.config):
115	            trial.add_hook(TrialEvent.AGENT_START, self._acquire_agent_permit)
116	            trial.add_hook(TrialEvent.AGENT_END, self._release_agent_permit)
117	            # Backstop cleanup for cancellation or hook failures after acquire.
118	            # It only releases permits held by this trial id.
119	            trial.add_hook(TrialEvent.END, self._release_all_agent_permits)
120	            trial.add_hook(TrialEvent.CANCEL, self._release_all_agent_permits)
121	
122	        for event, hooks in self._hooks.items():
123	            for hook in hooks:
124	                trial.add_hook(event, hook)
125	
126	    def _uses_agent_concurrency(self, trial_config: TrialConfig) -> bool:
127	        return trial_config.agent.n_concurrent is not None
128	
129	    def _agent_pool_key(self, trial_config: TrialConfig) -> str:
130	        return trial_config.agent.concurrency_key
131	
132	    def _get_agent_pool_semaphore(
133	        self, trial_config: TrialConfig
134	    ) -> asyncio.Semaphore | None:
135	        limit = trial_config.agent.n_concurrent
136	        if limit is None:
137	            return None
138	
139	        key = self._agent_pool_key(trial_config)
140	        existing_limit = self._agent_pool_limits.get(key)
141	        if existing_limit is not None and existing_limit != limit:
142	            raise ValueError(
143	                f"Conflicting n_concurrent values for agent concurrency pool {key!r}: "
144	                f"{existing_limit} and {limit}."
145	            )
146	
147	        self._agent_pool_limits[key] = limit
148	        if key not in self._agent_pool_semaphores:
149	            self._agent_pool_semaphores[key] = asyncio.Semaphore(limit)
150	        return self._agent_pool_semaphores[key]
151	
152	    def _validate_agent_concurrency(self, configs: list[TrialConfig]) -> None:
153	        for config in configs:
154	            self._get_agent_pool_semaphore(config)
155	
156	    async def _acquire_agent_permit(self, event: TrialHookEvent) -> None:
157	        semaphores = [
158	            semaphore
159	            for semaphore in (self._get_agent_pool_semaphore(event.config),)
160	            if semaphore is not None
161	        ]
162	        acquired: list[asyncio.Semaphore] = []
163	        try:
164	            for semaphore in semaphores:
165	                await semaphore.acquire()
166	                acquired.append(semaphore)
167	        except BaseException:
168	            for semaphore in reversed(acquired):
169	                semaphore.release()
170	            raise
171	
172	        if acquired:
173	            self._held_agent_permits.setdefault(event.trial_id, []).append(
174	                _AgentSemaphorePermit(semaphores=tuple(acquired))
175	            )
176	
177	    async def _release_agent_permit(self, event: TrialHookEvent) -> None:
178	        permits = self._held_agent_permits.get(event.trial_id)
179	        if not permits:
180	            return
181	
182	        permit = permits.pop()
183	        for semaphore in reversed(permit.semaphores):
184	            semaphore.release()
185	        if not permits:
186	            self._held_agent_permits.pop(event.trial_id, None)
187	
188	    async def _release_all_agent_permits(self, event: TrialHookEvent) -> None:
189	        permits = self._held_agent_permits.pop(event.trial_id, [])
190	        for permit in reversed(permits):
191	            for semaphore in reversed(permit.semaphores):
192	                semaphore.release()
193	
194	    async def _execute_trial_with_retries(
195	        self, trial_config: TrialConfig
196	    ) -> TrialResult:
197	        """Execute a trial with retry logic."""
198	        from harbor.trial.trial import Trial
199	
200	        for attempt in range(self._retry_config.max_retries + 1):
201	            trial = await Trial.create(trial_config)
202	            self._setup_hooks(trial)
203	            result = await trial.run()
204	
205	            if result.exception_info is None:
206	                return result
207	
208	            if not self._should_retry_exception(result.exception_info.exception_type):
209	                self._logger.debug(
210	                    "Not retrying trial because the exception is not in "
211	                    "include_exceptions or the maximum number of retries has been "
212	                    "reached"
213	                )
214	                return result
215	            if attempt == self._retry_config.max_retries:
216	                self._logger.debug(
217	                    "Not retrying trial because the maximum number of retries has been "
218	                    "reached"
219	                )
220	                return result
221	
222	            shutil.rmtree(trial.paths.trial_dir, ignore_errors=True)
223	
224	            delay_sec = self._calculate_backoff_delay_sec(attempt)
225	
226	            self._logger.debug(
227	                f"Trial {trial_config.trial_name} failed with exception "
228	                f"{result.exception_info.exception_type}. Retrying in "
229	                f"{delay_sec:.2f} seconds..."
230	            )
231	
232	            await asyncio.sleep(delay_sec)
233	
234	        raise RuntimeError(
235	            f"Trial {trial_config.trial_name} produced no result. This should never "
236	            "happen."
237	        )
238	
239	    async def _run_trial(self, trial_config: TrialConfig) -> TrialResult:
240	        """Execute a single trial, acquiring the semaphore for concurrency control."""
241	        async with self._semaphore:
242	            return await self._execute_trial_with_retries(trial_config)
243	
244	    def submit(self, trial_config: TrialConfig) -> Coroutine[Any, Any, TrialResult]:
245	        """
246	        Return a coroutine that executes one trial.
247	
248	        The caller decides how to schedule it (await, gather, TaskGroup).
249	        """
250	        self._validate_agent_concurrency([trial_config])
251	        return self._run_trial(trial_config)
252	
253	    def submit_batch(
254	        self, configs: list[TrialConfig]
255	    ) -> list[Coroutine[Any, Any, TrialResult]]:
256	        """
257	        Return coroutines for multiple trials, ordered to match `configs`.
258	        """
259	        self._validate_agent_concurrency(configs)
260	        return [self.submit(config) for config in configs]

71	class Trial(ABC):
72	    """Base trial lifecycle.
73	
74	    The base class owns setup, teardown, hooks, result persistence, and shared
75	    dependencies. Concrete subclasses own the workload shape.
76	    """
77	
78	    _AGENT_SETUP_TIMEOUT_SEC = 360
79	
80	    def __init__(
81	        self,
82	        config: TrialConfig,
83	        *,
84	        _task: Task | None = None,
85	    ):
86	        if _task is None:
87	            raise ValueError(
88	                "Instantiating Trial directly is deprecated. "
89	                "Use `await Trial.create(config)` instead."
90	            )
91	
92	        self.config = config
93	        self.job_id = config.job_id
94	        self.task = _task
95	
96	        self.paths = TrialPaths(trial_dir=(config.trials_dir / config.trial_name))
97	        self.paths.mkdir()
98	
99	        self.agent_env_paths = EnvironmentPaths.for_os(self.task.config.environment.os)
100	        self._injected_skills = self._resolve_injected_skills()
101	        self._effective_skills_dir = self._resolve_effective_skills_dir()
102	
103	        self._hooks: dict[TrialEvent, list[TrialHookCallback]] = {
104	            event: [] for event in TrialEvent
105	        }
106	
107	        self._are_agent_logs_downloaded = False
108	        self._is_agent_environment_stopped = False
109	        self._result: TrialResult | None = None
110	        self._log_handler: logging.Handler | None = None
111	        self._log_callbacks: list[LogCallback] = []
112	
113	        try:
114	            self._init_logger()
115	            self._init_timeouts()
116	            self._init_agent()
117	            self._init_agent_environment()
118	            self._init_artifact_handler()
119	            self._validate_network_policy_modes()
120	        except BaseException:
121	            self._close_logger_handler()
122	            raise
123	
124	    @property
125	    def result(self) -> TrialResult:
126	        if self._result is None:
127	            raise RuntimeError("Trial result accessed before initialization")
128	        return self._result
129	
130	    @staticmethod
131	    def _now() -> datetime:
132	        return datetime.now(timezone.utc)
133	
134	    def _network_plan(
135	        self,
136	        step_cfg: StepConfig | None = None,
137	        *,
138	        env_config: EnvironmentConfig | None = None,
139	    ) -> TrialNetworkPlan:
140	        verifier_mode = (
141	            resolve_step_verifier_mode(self.task.config, step_cfg)
142	            if step_cfg is not None
143	            else resolve_task_verifier_mode(self.task.config)
144	        )
145	        if env_config is not None:
146	            verifier_mode = VerifierEnvironmentMode.SEPARATE
147	        return resolve_trial_network_plan(
148	            self.task.config,
149	            self.config.agent,
150	            self.config.environment,
151	            step_cfg,
152	            verifier_mode=verifier_mode,
153	            env_config=env_config,
154	        )
155	
156	    def _validate_network_policy_modes(self) -> None:
157	        """Reject phase policies that require unsupported dynamic switching."""
158	        if not self.task.config.steps:
159	            self._validate_network_plan(self._network_plan(None), label="[agent]")
160	            return
161	
162	        for step in self.task.config.steps:
163	            self._validate_network_plan(
164	                self._network_plan(step),
165	                label=f"Step {step.name!r}",
166	            )
167	
168	    def _validate_network_plan(
169	        self,
170	        plan: TrialNetworkPlan,
171	        *,
172	        label: str,
173	    ) -> None:
174	        if plan.agent_phase != plan.agent_env_baseline:
175	            self._validate_dynamic_phase_switch(
176	                self.agent_environment,
177	                phase=plan.agent_phase,
178	                phase_label=f"{label} agent phase",
179	                environment_label="agent environment",
180	            )
181	
182	        if plan.verifier_phase != plan.verifier_phase_baseline:
183	            if plan.verifier_env_baseline is None:
184	                self._validate_dynamic_phase_switch(
185	                    self.agent_environment,
186	                    phase=plan.verifier_phase,
187	                    phase_label=f"{label} verifier phase",
188	                    environment_label="agent environment",
189	                )
190	            else:
191	                self.agent_environment.validate_network_policy_support(
192	                    plan.verifier_env_baseline
193	                )
194	                self._validate_dynamic_phase_switch(
195	                    self.agent_environment,
196	                    phase=plan.verifier_phase,
197	                    phase_label=f"{label} verifier phase",
198	                    environment_label="separate verifier environment",
199	                )
200	
201	    def _validate_dynamic_phase_switch(
202	        self,
203	        environment: BaseEnvironment,
204	        *,
205	        phase: NetworkPolicy,
206	        phase_label: str,
207	        environment_label: str,
208	    ) -> None:
209	        environment.validate_network_policy_support(phase)
210	        if not environment.capabilities.dynamic_network_policy:
211	            raise ValueError(
212	                f"{phase_label} network policy differs from the {environment_label} "
213	                "baseline, but this environment cannot change network policy after "
214	                "start."
215	            )
216	
217	    def _validate_separate_verifier_env_policies(
218	        self,
219	        environment: BaseEnvironment,
220	        *,
221	        plan: TrialNetworkPlan,
222	    ) -> None:
223	        if plan.verifier_env_baseline is None:
224	            raise RuntimeError(
225	                "separate verifier env validation requires a verifier baseline"
226	            )
227	        environment.validate_network_policy_support(plan.verifier_env_baseline)
228	
229	    @contextlib.asynccontextmanager
230	    async def _phase_network_policy(
231	        self,
232	        environment: BaseEnvironment,
233	        *,
234	        baseline_policy: NetworkPolicy,
235	        phase_policy: NetworkPolicy,
236	    ) -> AsyncGenerator[None, None]:
237	        if phase_policy == baseline_policy:
238	            yield
239	            return
240	
241	        await environment.set_network_policy(phase_policy)
242	        try:
243	            yield
244	        finally:
245	            await environment.set_network_policy(baseline_policy)
246	
247	    @classmethod
248	    async def create(cls, config: TrialConfig) -> "Trial":
249	        cls._resolve_agent_skills(config)
250	        task = await cls._load_task(config)
251	        if task.has_steps:
252	            from harbor.trial.multi_step import MultiStepTrial
253	
254	            return MultiStepTrial(config, _task=task)
255	
256	        from harbor.trial.single_step import SingleStepTrial
257	
258	        return SingleStepTrial(config, _task=task)
259	
260	    @staticmethod
261	    async def _load_task(config: TrialConfig) -> Task:
262	        if config.task.is_git_task() or config.task.is_package_task():
263	            client = TaskClient()
264	            task_id = config.task.get_task_id()
265	            task_dir = (
266	                await client.download_tasks(
267	                    task_ids=[task_id],
268	                    overwrite=config.task.overwrite,
269	                    output_dir=config.task.download_dir,
270	                )
271	            ).paths[0]
272	            return Task(
273	                task_dir=task_dir,
274	                extra_instruction_paths=config.extra_instruction_paths,
275	            )
276	
277	        if config.task.path is None:
278	            raise ValueError("Task path must be set for a local task.")
279	        return Task(
280	            task_dir=config.task.path,
281	            extra_instruction_paths=config.extra_instruction_paths,
282	        )
283	
284	    def add_hook(self, event: TrialEvent, hook: TrialHookCallback) -> None:
285	        self._hooks[event].append(hook)
286	
287	    def add_log_callback(self, callback: LogCallback) -> None:
288	        """Subscribe a callback to structured trial log streaming.
289	
290	        Appends to a list of subscribers, mirroring ``add_hook``.
291	        """
292	        self._log_callbacks.append(callback)
293	
294	    def _log_context(
295	        self,
296	        phase: LogPhase,
297	        environment: BaseEnvironment,
298	        step_name: str | None = None,
299	    ) -> contextlib.AbstractContextManager[None]:
300	        """Stream a phase's stdout/stderr chunks to the registered callbacks.
301	
302	        Returns a no-op context when nothing is subscribed, so the streaming
303	        path stays entirely opt-in and costs nothing by default.
304	        """
305	        callbacks = list(self._log_callbacks)
306	        if not callbacks:
307	            return contextlib.nullcontext()
308	
309	        async def emit(text: str, stream: OutputStream) -> None:
310	            entry = LogEntry(
311	                trial_id=self.config.trial_name,
312	                phase=phase,
313	                stream=stream,
314	                text=text,
315	                step_name=step_name,
316	            )
317	            for callback in callbacks:
318	                await callback(entry)
319	
320	        return environment.scoped_output_callback(emit)
321	
322	    async def _emit(self, event: TrialEvent) -> None:
323	        hook_event = TrialHookEvent(
324	            event=event,
325	            trial_id=self.config.trial_name,
326	            task_name=self.task.name,
327	            config=self.config,
328	            result=self._result,
329	        )
330	        for hook in self._hooks[event]:
331	            await hook(hook_event)
332	
333	    async def run(self) -> TrialResult:
334	        self._init_result()
335	        await self._emit(TrialEvent.START)
336	
337	        try:
338	            await self._prepare()
339	            if not self.config.install_only:
340	                # Setup/install ran in _prepare(); skip the agent run + verification.
341	                await self._run()
342	        except asyncio.CancelledError as exc:
343	            self.logger.debug(f"Trial {self.config.trial_name} cancelled")
344	            self._record_exception(exc)
345	            await self._recover_outputs()
346	            await self._emit(TrialEvent.CANCEL)
347	            raise
348	        except Exception as exc:
349	            self.logger.debug(f"Trial {self.config.trial_name} failed: {exc}")
350	            self._record_exception(exc)
351	            await self._recover_outputs()
352	        finally:
353	            await self._finalize()
354	            self._close_logger_handler()
355	
356	        return self.result
357	
358	    @abstractmethod
359	    async def _run(self) -> None:
360	        pass
361	
362	    @abstractmethod
363	    async def _recover_outputs(self) -> None:
364	        pass
365	
366	    async def _prepare(self) -> None:
367	        await self._setup_agent_environment()
368	        await self.agent_environment.run_healthcheck()
369	        await self._upload_injected_skills()
370	        with self.agent_environment.with_default_user(self.task.config.agent.user):
371	            await self._setup_agent()
372	        self.result.agent_info = self.agent.to_agent_info()
373	
374	    async def _finalize(self) -> None:
375	        await self._stop_agent_environment()
376	        self.result.finished_at = self._now()
377	        self.paths.result_path.write_text(self.result.model_dump_json(indent=4))
378	        await self._emit(TrialEvent.END)
379	
380	    def _record_exception(self, exc: BaseException) -> None:
381	        if self.result.exception_info is not None:
382	            self.logger.debug(
383	                "Skipping exception record because trial already has exception_info",
384	                exc_info=(type(exc), exc, exc.__traceback__),
385	            )
386	            return
387	
388	        self.result.exception_info = ExceptionInfo.from_exception(exc)
389	        self.paths.exception_message_path.write_text(traceback.format_exc())
390	
391	    def _resolve_timeout_sec(
392	        self,
393	        *,
394	        base_sec: float,
395	        max_sec: float | None = None,
396	        multiplier: float | None,
397	    ) -> float:
398	        resolved_multiplier = (
399	            multiplier if multiplier is not None else self.config.timeout_multiplier
400	        )
401	        return min(base_sec, max_sec or float("inf")) * resolved_multiplier
402	
403	    async def _run_agent_phase(
404	        self,
405	        *,
406	        target: TrialResult | StepResult,
407	        instruction: str,
408	        timeout_sec: float | None,
409	        user: str | int | None,
410	        step_cfg: StepConfig | None = None,
411	    ) -> None:
412	        await self._emit(TrialEvent.AGENT_START)
413	
414	        target.agent_result = AgentContext()
415	        target.agent_execution = TimingInfo(started_at=self._now())
416	
417	        try:
418	            plan = self._network_plan(step_cfg)
419	            step_name = step_cfg.name if step_cfg is not None else None
420	            with self.agent_environment.with_default_user(user):
421	                async with self._phase_network_policy(
422	                    self.agent_environment,
423	                    baseline_policy=plan.agent_env_baseline,
424	                    phase_policy=plan.agent_phase,
425	                ):
426	                    with self.agent_environment.scoped_exec_env(self.agent.extra_env):
427	                        with self._log_context(
428	                            "agent", self.agent_environment, step_name
429	                        ):
430	                            await asyncio.wait_for(
431	                                self.agent.run(
432	                                    instruction=instruction,
433	                                    environment=self.agent_environment,
434	                                    context=target.agent_result,
435	                                ),
436	                                timeout=timeout_sec,
437	                            )
438	        except asyncio.TimeoutError as exc:
439	            raise AgentTimeoutError(
440	                f"Agent execution timed out after {timeout_sec} seconds"
441	            ) from exc
442	        finally:
443	            target.agent_execution.finished_at = self._now()
444	            await self._emit(TrialEvent.AGENT_END)
445	
446	    async def _download_agent_logs(self) -> None:
447	        if self._are_agent_logs_downloaded:
448	            return
449	
450	        if self.agent_environment.capabilities.mounted:
451	            await self.agent_environment.prepare_logs_for_host()
452	            self._are_agent_logs_downloaded = True
453	            return
454	
455	        try:
456	            if self.config.agent.include_logs or self.config.agent.exclude_logs:
457	                await self.agent_environment.download_dir_filtered(
458	                    source_dir=self.agent_env_paths.agent_dir.as_posix(),
459	                    target_dir=self.paths.agent_dir,
460	                    include=self.config.agent.include_logs or None,
461	                    exclude=self.config.agent.exclude_logs or None,
462	                )
463	            else:
464	                await self.agent_environment.download_dir(
465	                    source_dir=self.agent_env_paths.agent_dir.as_posix(),
466	                    target_dir=self.paths.agent_dir,
467	                )
468	        except Exception:
469	            self.logger.error(f"Failed to download logs to {self.paths.agent_dir}")
470	
471	        self._are_agent_logs_downloaded = True
472	
473	    async def _upload_agent_logs(self) -> None:
474	        """Upload locally-generated agent logs back to non-mounted environments."""
475	        if self.agent_environment.capabilities.mounted:
476	            return
477	
478	        try:
479	            await self.agent_environment.upload_dir(
480	                source_dir=self.paths.agent_dir,
481	                target_dir=self.agent_env_paths.agent_dir.as_posix(),
482	            )
483	        except Exception:
484	            self.logger.error("Failed to upload agent logs back to environment")
485	
486	    async def _run_shared_verifier(
487	        self,
488	        *,
489	        timeout_sec: float | None,
490	        user: str | int | None,
491	        env: dict[str, str] | None = None,
492	        step_name: str | None = None,
493	        step_cfg: StepConfig | None = None,
494	    ) -> VerifierResult:
495	        plan = self._network_plan(step_cfg)
496	        with self.agent_environment.with_default_user(user):
497	            verifier = VerifierFactory.create_verifier_from_config(
498	                self.config.verifier,
499	                task=self.task,
500	                trial_paths=self.paths,
501	                environment=self.agent_environment,
502	                override_env=self.config.verifier.env or None,
503	                logger=self.logger,
504	                verifier_env=env,
505	                step_name=step_name,
506	            )
507	            async with self._phase_network_policy(
508	                self.agent_environment,
509	                baseline_policy=plan.agent_env_baseline,
510	                phase_policy=plan.verifier_phase,
511	            ):
512	                with self._log_context(
513	                    "verification", self.agent_environment, step_name
514	                ):
515	                    return await asyncio.wait_for(
516	                        verifier.verify(), timeout=timeout_sec
517	                    )
518	
519	    async def _run_separate_verifier(
520	        self,
521	        *,
522	        key: str,
523	        timeout_sec: float | None,
524	        artifacts_dir: Path,
525	        artifacts: Sequence[str | ArtifactConfig] | None = None,
526	        step_cfg: StepConfig | None = None,
527	        user: str | int | None,
528	        env: dict[str, str] | None = None,
529	    ) -> VerifierResult:
530	        env_config = resolve_effective_verifier_env_config(self.task.config, step_cfg)
531	
532	        if env_config is None:
533	            message = "Separate verifier mode did not resolve an environment config"
534	            if step_cfg is not None:
535	                message += f" for step {step_cfg.name!r}. This should never happen."
536	            raise RuntimeError(message)
537	
538	        plan = self._network_plan(step_cfg, env_config=env_config)
539	
540	        async with self._separate_verifier_env(
541	            env_config,
542	            key=key,
543	            plan=plan,
544	            step_cfg=step_cfg,
545	        ) as target_env:
546	            with target_env.with_default_user(user):
547	                env_paths = EnvironmentPaths.for_os(target_env.os)
548	
549	                await target_env.empty_dirs([env_paths.verifier_dir], chmod=True)
550	
551	                await self._artifact_handler.upload_artifacts(
552	                    target_env,
553	                    artifacts_dir=artifacts_dir,
554	                    source_artifacts_dir=self.agent_env_paths.artifacts_dir,
555	                    target_artifacts_dir=env_paths.artifacts_dir,
556	                    artifacts=artifacts,
557	                )
558	
559	                verifier = VerifierFactory.create_verifier_from_config(
560	                    self.config.verifier,
561	                    task=self.task,
562	                    trial_paths=self.paths,
563	                    environment=target_env,
564	                    override_env=self.config.verifier.env or None,
565	                    logger=self.logger,
566	                    verifier_env=env,
567	                    step_name=step_cfg.name if step_cfg is not None else None,
568	                    skip_tests_upload=True,
569	                )
570	
571	                verifier_env_baseline = plan.verifier_env_baseline
572	                if verifier_env_baseline is None:
573	                    raise RuntimeError(
574	                        "separate verifier env requires a verifier baseline "
575	                        "in the network plan"
576	                    )
577	
578	                step_name = step_cfg.name if step_cfg is not None else None
579	                async with self._phase_network_policy(
580	                    target_env,
581	                    baseline_policy=verifier_env_baseline,
582	                    phase_policy=plan.verifier_phase,
583	                ):
584	                    with self._log_context("verification", target_env, step_name):
585	                        return await asyncio.wait_for(
586	                            verifier.verify(), timeout=timeout_sec
587	                        )
588	
589	    @contextlib.asynccontextmanager
590	    async def _separate_verifier_env(
591	        self,
592	        env_config: EnvironmentConfig,
593	        *,
594	        key: str,
595	        plan: TrialNetworkPlan,
596	        step_cfg: StepConfig | None = None,
597	    ) -> AsyncGenerator[BaseEnvironment, None]:
598	        verifier_runtime_config = self.config.environment.model_copy(
599	            update={"extra_docker_compose": []}
600	        )
601	        if plan.verifier_env_baseline is None:
602	            raise RuntimeError(
603	                "separate verifier env requires a verifier baseline in the network plan"
604	            )
605	        env = EnvironmentFactory.create_environment_from_config(
606	            config=verifier_runtime_config,
607	            environment_dir=self._verifier_env_build_context(step_cfg),
608	            environment_name=self.task.short_name,
609	            session_id=self._separate_verifier_session_id(key),
610	            trial_paths=self.paths,
611	            task_env_config=env_config,
612	            logger=self.logger,
613	            mounts=self._verifier_env_mounts(env_config),
614	            network_policy=plan.verifier_env_baseline,
615	        )
616	        self._validate_separate_verifier_env_policies(env, plan=plan)
617	        try:
618	            await asyncio.wait_for(
619	                env.start(force_build=False),
620	                timeout=self._environment_build_timeout_sec,
621	            )
622	            yield env
623	        finally:
624	            try:
625	                await asyncio.shield(env.stop(delete=self.config.environment.delete))
626	            except Exception as exc:
627	                self.logger.debug(f"Failed to stop verifier env '{key}': {exc}")
628	
629	    def _verifier_env_mounts(
630	        self,
631	        env_config: EnvironmentConfig,
632	    ) -> list[ServiceVolumeConfig]:
633	        env_paths = EnvironmentPaths.for_os(env_config.os)
634	        return [
635	            ServiceVolumeConfig(
636	                type="bind",
637	                source=self.paths.verifier_dir.resolve().absolute().as_posix(),
638	                target=str(env_paths.verifier_dir),
639	            )
640	        ]
641	
642	    def _verifier_env_build_context(
643	        self,
644	        step_cfg: StepConfig | None,
645	    ) -> Path:
646	        if step_cfg is not None:
647	            step_tests_dir = self.task.paths.step_tests_dir(step_cfg.name)
648	            if step_tests_dir.exists():
649	                return step_tests_dir
650	        return self.task.paths.tests_dir
651	
652	    def _separate_verifier_session_id(self, key: str) -> str:
653	        raw = f"{self.config.trial_name}__verifier__{key}"
654	        safe = "".join(char if char.isalnum() or char in "-._" else "_" for char in raw)
655	        if len(safe) <= _MAX_VERIFIER_ENV_SESSION_ID_LEN:
656	            return safe
657	
658	        digest = hashlib.sha1(safe.encode()).hexdigest()[:8]
659	        suffix = f"__{digest}"
660	        prefix = safe[: _MAX_VERIFIER_ENV_SESSION_ID_LEN - len(suffix)].rstrip("-._")
661	        return f"{prefix}{suffix}"
662	
663	    def _populate_agent_context(self, agent_result: AgentContext | None) -> None:
664	        if agent_result is None or not agent_result.is_empty():
665	            return
666	
667	        self.agent.populate_context_post_run(agent_result)
668	
669	    async def _sync_agent_output(self, target: TrialResult | StepResult) -> None:
670	        await self._download_agent_logs()
671	        self._populate_agent_context(target.agent_result)
672	
673	    def _init_result(self) -> None:
674	        self.paths.trial_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
675	        self.paths.config_path.write_text(self.config.model_dump_json(indent=4))
676	        self._result = TrialResult(
677	            trial_name=self.config.trial_name,
678	            task_name=self.task.name,
679	            task_id=self.config.task.get_task_id(),
680	            started_at=self._now(),
681	            config=self.config,
682	            task_checksum=self.task.checksum,
683	            trial_uri=self.paths.trial_dir.expanduser().resolve().as_uri(),
684	            agent_info=self.agent.to_agent_info(),
685	            source=self.config.task.source,
686	        )
687	
688	    def _init_logger(self) -> None:
689	        self.logger = global_logger.getChild(f"{__name__}.{self.config.trial_name}")
690	        file_handler = logging.FileHandler(self.paths.log_path)
691	        file_handler.setLevel(logging.DEBUG)
692	        self.logger.addHandler(file_handler)
693	        self._log_handler = file_handler
694	
695	    def _close_logger_handler(self) -> None:
696	        if self._log_handler is None:
697	            return
698	
699	        self.logger.removeHandler(self._log_handler)
700	        self._log_handler.close()
701	        self._log_handler = None
702	
703	    def _init_agent(self) -> None:

812	        entries = [

816	                artifact

818	                for artifact in step.artifacts

827	    def _all_collect_hooks(self) -> list[VerifierCollectConfig]:

842	    async def _run_collect_hooks(
843	        self,
844	        hooks: Sequence[VerifierCollectConfig],
845	    ) -> None:
846	        """Run collect hooks best-effort; failures never abort the trial."""
847	        for hook in hooks:
848	            self.logger.debug(
849	                f"Running collect hook in service '{hook.service}': {hook.command!r}"
850	            )
851	            try:
852	                result = await self.agent_environment.service_exec(
853	                    hook.command,
854	                    service=hook.service,
855	                    timeout_sec=int(hook.timeout_sec),
856	                    user=hook.user,
857	                )
858	                if result.return_code != 0:
859	                    self.logger.warning(
860	                        f"Collect hook in service '{hook.service}' exited with "
861	                        f"code {result.return_code}: {hook.command!r}. "
862	                        f"stdout: {result.stdout} stderr: {result.stderr}"
863	                    )
864	                else:
865	                    self.logger.debug(
866	                        f"Collect hook in service '{hook.service}' completed"
867	                    )
868	            except Exception as exc:
869	                self.logger.warning(
870	                    f"Collect hook in service '{hook.service}' failed "
871	                    f"({hook.command!r}): {exc}"
872	                )

874	    async def _collect_artifacts_phased(
875	        self,
876	        *,
877	        artifacts_dir: Path,
878	        step_cfg: StepConfig | None = None,
879	        step_artifacts: Sequence[str | ArtifactConfig] | None = None,
880	        stop_main_before_sidecars: bool = False,
881	    ) -> None:
882	        """Collect artifacts in two passes: main first, then sidecar services.
883	
884	        Sidecar evidence is collected over a channel the agent cannot write
885	        to (each service's own filesystem). When *stop_main_before_sidecars*
886	        is set (separate verifier mode, last use of the agent env), the main
887	        service is stopped before the sidecar pass so leftover agent
888	        processes cannot interfere with collection.
889	        """
890	        hooks = self._collect_hooks_for(step_cfg)
891	        main_hooks = [hook for hook in hooks if hook.service == MAIN_SERVICE_NAME]
892	        sidecar_hooks = [hook for hook in hooks if hook.service != MAIN_SERVICE_NAME]
893	
894	        # Claims dedupe within this pass only; prior steps' host paths were
895	        # archived away, so their claims must not skip this pass's entries.
896	        self._artifact_handler.begin_collection()
897	
898	        self.logger.debug("Collecting main service artifacts")
899	        await self._run_collect_hooks(main_hooks)
900	        await self._artifact_handler.download_artifacts(
901	            self.agent_environment,
902	            artifacts_dir,
903	            source_artifacts_dir=self.agent_env_paths.artifacts_dir,
904	            artifacts=step_artifacts,
905	            services={MAIN_SERVICE_NAME},
906	        )
907	
908	        sidecars = self._artifact_handler.sidecar_services(step_artifacts)
909	        sidecars |= {hook.service for hook in sidecar_hooks}
910	        if not sidecars:
911	            return
912	
913	        if stop_main_before_sidecars:
914	            self.logger.debug(
915	                "Stopping main service before sidecar evidence collection"
916	            )
917	            try:
918	                await self.agent_environment.stop_service(MAIN_SERVICE_NAME)
919	                self.logger.debug("Main service stopped")
920	            except Exception as exc:
921	                self.logger.warning(
922	                    f"Failed to stop main service before sidecar collection: {exc}"
923	                )
924	
925	        self.logger.debug(
926	            f"Collecting sidecar artifacts from services: {sorted(sidecars)}"
927	        )
928	        await self._run_collect_hooks(sidecar_hooks)
929	        await self._artifact_handler.download_artifacts(
930	            self.agent_environment,
931	            artifacts_dir,
932	            source_artifacts_dir=self.agent_env_paths.artifacts_dir,
933	            artifacts=step_artifacts,
934	            services=sidecars,
935	        )
